item.title: Having a Baby? 5 Tips to Avoid Problems From Flawed Drug Testing at the Hospital
item.description: Hospitals routinely use unreliable drug tests at childbirth, and many parents are reported to child welfare agencies and the police. Here’s what you c...

item.title: Iowa’s Top Court Ended Court Fees in Dismissed Cases. Politicians Want Them Back
item.description: The state can no longer saddle defendants with court fees when charges are dismissed. Prosecutors are backing legislation to restore them.

item.title: The Marshall Project: Diversity, Equity and Inclusion, 2025
item.description: Our ninth annual diversity report notes developments in 2025 and lays out goals for the year ahead.

item.title: Journalists: New Data to Investigate the Consequences of Hospital Drug Testing at Childbirth
item.description: We collected child welfare data in 21 states to report on the consequences of faulty drug tests for pregnant women, including referrals to law enforce...

item.title: After Disputed Idaho Raid, Lawsuit Tries Different Approach to Hold ICE Accountable
item.description: It’s nearly impossible to sue federal officials for civil rights violations, but a new ACLU filing could become a test case for how to do it.
